LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MAY 17, 2013. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-17-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION. FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED A DUTY OWED BY THE DEFENDANT TO THE PLAINTIFF OR A BREACH OF THAT DUTY. (IN RE COOPER/T. SMITH (5TH CIR. 1991) 929 F.2D 1073, 1077; COMPLAINT 13, 28, 31. FOR THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT RAISES QUESTIONS OF FACT REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S RELATIONSHIP TO DEFENDANT THAT ARE NOT THE PROPER SUBJECT OF A DEMURRER. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND ONLY TO ALLEGE A DUTY OWED BY THE DEFENDANT TO THE PLAINTIFF AND A BREACH OF THAT DUTY. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO ADD A SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN HIS OPPOSITION IS DENIED AS PROCEDURALLY INAPPROPRIATE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE FROM 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT PARAGRAPH 13(G); "CALLOUSLY AND RECKLESSLY" FROM PARAGRAPH 14; "RECKLESS" FROM PARAGRAPH 21, AND PARAGRAPH 5 ON PAGE 9 (IN PRAYER FOR RELIEF) IS DENIED. SEE BAPTISTE V. SUPERIOR COURT (1980) 106 CAL.APP.3D 87, 96, 100, 104 (JONES ACT); ATLANTIC SOUNDING CO. INC. V. TOWNSEND (2009) 557 U.S. 404, 415 (UNSEAWORTHINESS); EVICH V. MORRIS (9TH CIR. 1987) 819 F.2D 256, 258 ("PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE AVAILABLE UNDER GENERAL MARITIME LAW FOR CLAIMS OF UNSEAWORTHINESS"); ROWE V. HORNBLOWER FLEET (N.D.CAL. 2012) 2012 WL 5833541 ("NO PROVISIONS IN THE JONES ACT THAT LIMIT THE RIGHT TO SEEK PUNITIVE DAMAGES ON A CLAIM FOR UNSEAWORTHINESS"). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DENYING MOTION TO STRIKE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM PLAINTIFFS SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT - AS A PRELIMINARY MATTER, THE COURT VACATES ITS MARCH 13, 2015 ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION AS UNOPPOSED. APPARENTLY DEFENDANT FILED ITS MOTION TWICE WITH TWO DIFFERENT HEARING DATES. WHILE PLAINTIFF DID NOT FILE AN OPPOSITION TO THE FIRST MOTION, PLAINTIFF DID FILE AN OPPOSITION TO THIS SUBSEQUENTLY FILED MOTION. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT VACATES ITS PREVIOUS ORDER IN FAVOR OF RULING ON THE MERITS OF THE MOTION. DEFENDANT'S RENEWED MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 1008(B) IS DENIED. THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") ONLY SEEKS PUNITIVE DAMAGES UNDER THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WILLFUL AND CAPRICIOUS FAILURE TO PAY MAINTENANCE, FOUND, AND CURE. (SEE SAC, P. 9, PAR. 5; SAC, P. 7, PARS. 26-27.) PER ATLANTIC SOUNDING CO., INC. V. TOWNSEND (2009) 557 U.S. 404, 424-25, PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE AVAILABLE FOR THE WILLFUL AND WANTON DISREGARD OF THE MAINTENANCE AND CURE OBLIGATION. THUS, THE MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPH 5 FROM THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF IS DENIED. DEFENDANT ALSO SEEKS TO STRIKE REFERENCES TO RECKLESS BEHAVIOR UNDER THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THIS LANGUAGE IS DENIED. BECAUSE THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF ONLY SEEKS PUNITIVE DAMAGES UNDER THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, STRIKING THE ALLEGATIONS OF RECKLESSNESS FROM THE FIRST TWO CAUSES OF ACTION WOULD SERVE NO PRACTICAL PURPOSE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: IRENE L. TOFFT, CSR # 12913 (302/HK)
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-24-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CALVI'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE PROPOSED NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CLAIM IS NOT TIME BARRED. FIRST, THE 3 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPLIES. SECOND, EVEN IF THE COURT APPLIED STATE LAW, THE CLAIM WOULD BE TIMELY UNDER THE RELATION BACK DOCTRINE. Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DEREK B. JACOBSON OF THE McGUINN, HILLSMAN & PALEFSKY, TEL. NO. 1-415-421-9292 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BARRY W. PONTICELLO, TEL. NO. 1-619-255-6450 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CALVI'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted. The proposed negligent misrepresentation claim is not time barred. First, the 3 year statute of limitations applies. Second, even if the court applied state law, the claim would be timely under the relation back doctrine. The prejudice to defendant is minimal given the current trial date and because discovery remains open. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-27-2015 Continued To Nov-09-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Strike Claim For Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Renee C. St. Clair In Support Thereof; [Proposed] Order Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Strike Claim For Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint - As a preliminary matter, the Court vacates its March 13, 2015 Order granting Defendant's motion as unopposed. Apparently Defendant filed its motion twice with two different hearing dates. While Plaintiff did not file an opposition to the first motion, Plaintiff did file an opposition to this subsequently filed motion. Accordingly, the Court vacates its previous order in favor of ruling on the merits of the motion. Defendant's renewed motion to strike pursuant to CCP sec. 1008(b) is denied. The prayer for relief in the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") only seeks punitive damages under the third cause of action for Willful and Capricious Failure to Pay Maintenance, Found, and Cure. (See SAC, p. 9, par. 5; SAC, p. 7, pars. 26-27.) Per Atlantic Sounding Co., Inc. v. Townsend (2009) 557 U.S. 404, 424-25, punitive damages are available for the willful and wanton disregard of the maintenance and cure obligation. Thus, the motion to strike paragraph 5 from the prayer for relief is denied. Defendant also seeks to strike references to reckless behavior under the first and second causes of action. The motion to strike this language is denied. Because the prayer for relief only seeks punitive damages under the third cause of action, striking the allegations of recklessness from the first two causes of action would serve no practical purpose. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR # 12913 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOHN HILLSMAN, ESQ., 415.531.7136, FOR PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CALVI.
BARRY PONTICELLO, ESQ., 619.255.6450, FOR DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC'S MOTION to strike FROM 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT paragraph 13(g); "callously and recklessly" from paragraph 14; "reckless" from paragraph 21, and paragraph 5 on page 9 (in prayer for relief) is denied. See Baptiste v. Superior Court (1980) 106 Cal.App.3d 87, 96, 100, 104 (Jones Act); Atlantic Sounding Co. Inc. v. Townsend (2009) 557 U.S. 404, 415 (unseaworthiness); Evich v. Morris (9th Cir. 1987) 819 F.2d 256, 258 ("Punitive damages are available under general maritime law for claims of unseaworthiness"); Rowe v. Hornblower Fleet (N.D.Cal. 2012) 2012 WL 5833541 ("no provisions in the Jones Act that limit the right to seek punitive damages on a claim for unseaworthiness"). Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Off Calendar Jun-17-2013 Continued To Aug-21-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-17-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT SILVER BAY SEAFOODS LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action and overruled as to the fifth cause of action. For the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff has not alleged a duty owed by the defendant to the plaintiff or a breach of that duty. (In re Cooper/T. Smith (5th Cir. 1991) 929 F.2d 1073, 1077; Complaint 13, 28, 31. For the fifth cause of action, Defendant raises questions of fact regarding Plaintiff's relationship to Defendant that are not the proper subject of a demurrer. Plaintiff is given leave to amend only to allege a duty owed by the defendant to the plaintiff and a breach of that duty. Plaintiff's request to add a sixth cause of action in his opposition is denied as procedurally inappropriate. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/RAK)
